title: Cache Controller Read Operation
description: Pseudocode for handling a read request in a cache controller
code:
READ_OPERATION(address)
BEGIN
 index = address[INDEX_BITS]
 offset = address[OFFSET_BITS]
 
 // Check if cache line is valid and tag matches
 IF cache[index].valid == TRUE AND cache[index].tag == address[TAG_BITS] THEN
 // Cache Hit
 data = cache[index].data[offset]
 UPDATE_LRU(index)
 RETURN data
 ELSE
 // Cache Miss
 victim_line = SELECT_VICTIM(index)
 
 // Write back if dirty
 IF cache[victim_line].dirty == TRUE THEN
 WRITE_BACK(cache[victim_line].tag, cache[victim_line].data)
 END IF
 
 // Fetch from memory
 new_block = FETCH_FROM_MEMORY(address)
 
 // Update cache
 cache[victim_line].data = new_block
 cache[victim_line].tag = address[TAG_BITS]
 cache[victim_line].valid = TRUE
 cache[victim_line].dirty = FALSE
 UPDATE_LRU(victim_line)
 
 RETURN new_block[offset]
 END IF
END
explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the complete read operation flow - checking for hit, handling miss by selecting victim, writing back if dirty, fetching from memory, and updating cache.

complexity.time: O(1) for hit, O(1) for miss (constant time operations)

title: LRU Replacement Algorithm
description: Implementation of LRU replacement policy using counter array
code:
INITIALIZE_LRU(associativity)
BEGIN
 FOR each set DO
 FOR each way DO
 lru_counter[set][way] = way
 END FOR
 END FOR
END

UPDATE_LRU(set, accessed_way)
BEGIN
 // The accessed way becomes most recently used
 // All ways with higher counter get decremented
 accessed_value = lru_counter[set][accessed_way]
 
 FOR each way IN set DO
 IF lru_counter[set][way] > accessed_value THEN
 lru_counter[set][way] = lru_counter[set][way] - 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 lru_counter[set][accessed_way] = associativity - 1
END

SELECT_VICTIM_LRU(set)
BEGIN
 // Find way with lowest counter (least recently used)
 min_counter = INFINITY
 victim_way = 0
 
 FOR each way IN set DO
 IF lru_counter[set][way] < min_counter THEN
 min_counter = lru_counter[set][way]
 victim_way = way
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN victim_way
END
explanation: This shows a simple LRU implementation using counters. Each access updates counters so that higher values indicate more recently used lines. The victim is the one with the lowest counter value.

complexity.time: O(associativity) per update, O(associativity) per selection

title: MESI Protocol State Machine
description: State transitions for MESI cache coherence protocol
code:
// MESI State Machine for Cache Line
// Events: BusRd (Bus Read), BusRdX (Bus Read Exclusive), Flush, Invalidate

STATE_TRANSITIONS(line_state, event)
BEGIN
 SWITCH line_state
 CASE INVALID:
 IF event == BusRd THEN
 RETURN SHARED // Other cache has it, share
 ELSE IF event == BusRdX THEN
 RETURN INVALID // Request exclusive access
 ELSE IF event == Local_Read THEN
 BusRd // Issue bus read
 RETURN SHARED
 ELSE IF event == Local_Write THEN
 BusRdX // Issue bus read exclusive
 RETURN MODIFIED
 END IF
 
 CASE SHARED:
 IF event == Local_Write THEN
 RETURN MODIFIED // Become exclusive
 ELSE IF event == BusRdX THEN
 RETURN INVALID // Another wants exclusive
 ELSE IF event == Invalidate THEN
 RETURN INVALID
 END IF
 
 CASE MODIFIED:
 IF event == BusRd THEN
 WRITE_BACK(line.data) // Write back, give copy
 RETURN SHARED
 ELSE IF event == BusRdX THEN
 WRITE_BACK(line.data)
 RETURN INVALID
 END IF
 
 CASE EXCLUSIVE:
 IF event == BusRd THEN
 RETURN SHARED
 ELSE IF event == Local_Write THEN
 RETURN MODIFIED
 END IF
 END SWITCH
 
 RETURN line_state
END
explanation: This demonstrates the MESI protocol state machine that maintains cache coherence in multi-processor systems. Each state transition occurs based on local events and bus transactions from other processors.

title: Write Buffer Operation
description: Handling write operations with a write buffer
code:
WRITE_BUFFER_WRITE(address, data)
BEGIN
 // Check if address already in write buffer
 entry = FIND_IN_BUFFER(address)
 
 IF entry != NULL THEN
 // Update existing entry
 entry.data = data
 entry.dirty = TRUE
 ELSE
 // Add new entry to buffer
 IF buffer_full THEN
 // Wait for space or drain oldest entry
 DRAIN_OLDEST_ENTRY()
 END IF
 
 buffer[buffer_tail].address = address
 buffer[buffer_tail].data = data
 buffer[buffer_tail].dirty = TRUE
 buffer_tail = (buffer_tail + 1) % BUFFER_SIZE
 END IF
 
 // Write to cache (write-back) or main memory (write-through)
 CACHE_WRITE(address, data)
END

DRAIN_WRITE_BUFFER()
BEGIN
 WHILE buffer_head != buffer_tail DO
 entry = buffer[buffer_head]
 WRITE_TO_MEMORY(entry.address, entry.data)
 buffer_head = (buffer_head + 1) % BUFFER_SIZE
 END WHILE
END
explanation: This shows how a write buffer works - writes are queued and the processor continues without waiting. The buffer is drained to main memory asynchronously.

complexity.time: O(1) for buffer insert, O(n) for drain
